description: country in West Asia
description_wiki: Israel, officially the State of Israel, is a country in the Southern Levant region of West Asia.

description: sovereign state in northeastern Europe
description_wiki: Latvia, officially the Republic of Latvia, is a country in the Baltic region of northern Europe.

description: Israeli politician, statesman and general (1922–1995)
description_wiki: Yitzhak Rabin (; Hebrew: יִצְחָק רַבִּין, IPA: [itsˈχak ˈʁabin]; 1 March 1922 – 4 November 1995) was an Israeli statesman and general who was the prime minister of Israel, having served two terms in office, 1974–1977, and from 1992 until his assassination in 1995.

description: city in Israel
description_wiki: Tel Aviv, officially Tel Aviv-Yafo, and also known as Tel Aviv-Jaffa, is the most populous city in the Gush Dan metropolitan area of Israel.

description: sea between Europe, Africa and Asia
description_wiki: The Mediterranean Sea ( MED-ih-tə-RAY-nee-ən) is an intercontinental sea situated between Europe, Asia, and Africa.

description: geopolitical region encompassing Egypt and most of Western Asia, including Iran
description_wiki: The Middle East is a geopolitical region encompassing the Arabian Peninsula, Egypt, Iran, Iraq, the Levant, and Turkey.

description: Northwest Semitic language
description_wiki: Hebrew is a Northwest Semitic language within the Afroasiatic language family.

description: country in Western Europe and other continents (through its overseas territories in America, Africa and Oceania)
description_wiki: France, officially the French Republic, is a country primarily located in Western Europe.

description: France based hotel and restaurant guide
description_wiki: The Michelin Guide ( MISH-əl-in, MITCH-əl-in; French: Guide Michelin [ɡid miʃlɛ̃]) is a restaurant guide that has been published by the French tire company Michelin since 1900.

description: large region mainly located in northern Israel
description_wiki: Galilee (; Hebrew: הַגָּלִיל, romanized: hag-Gālīl; Ancient Greek: Galilaia, lit. 'Γαλιλαία'; Latin: Galilaea; Arabic: الجليل, romanized: al-Jalīl) is a region located in northern Israel and southern Lebanon consisting of two parts: the Upper Galilee (הגליל העליון, hag-Galil ha-ʿElyon; الجليل الأعلى, al-Jalīl al-Aʿlā) and the Lower Galilee (הגליל התחתון, hag-Galil hat-Taḥton; الجليل الأسفل, al-Jalīl al-Asfal).

description: family name
description_wiki: Asif (Arabic: آصف) is an Arabic masculine given name.

description: city in the Middle East, holy to the three Abrahamic religions
description_wiki: Jerusalem is a city in the Southern Levant, on a plateau in the Judaean Mountains between the Mediterranean and the Dead Sea.

description: country located primarily in North America
description_wiki: The United States of America (USA), also known as the United States (U.S.) or America, is a country primarily located in North America.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: California () is a state in the Western United States that lies on the Pacific Coast.

description: region in central Italy
description_wiki: Tuscany ( TUSK-ə-nee; Italian: Toscana [tosˈkaːna]) is an administrative region of central Italy with an area of about 23,000 square kilometres (8,900 square miles) and a population of 3,660,834 as of 2025.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Kentucky (US:  , UK: ), officially the Commonwealth of Kentucky, is a landlocked state in the Southeastern region of the United States.

description: yellow citron
description_wiki: Etrog (Hebrew: אֶתְרוֹג, plural: etrogim; Ashkenazi Hebrew: esrog, plural: esrogim), also spelled esrog is the yellow citron (Citrus medica) used by Jews during the weeklong holiday of Sukkot as one of the four species.

description: Jewish Holiday, Harvest Festival, Festival of Booths
description_wiki: Sukkot, also known as the Feast of Tabernacles or Feast of Booths, is a Torah-commanded Jewish holiday celebrated for seven days, beginning on the 15th day of the month of Tishrei.

description: miscellaneous by House of Fabergé (Russian, 1842-1918); Johan Viktor Aarne (Finnish, 1863-1934) (1966.484)

aliases: The City So Nice They Named It Twice
description: most populous city in the United States
description_wiki: New York, often called New York City (NYC), is the most populous city in the United States.

description: capital and largest city of England and the United Kingdom
description_wiki: London is the capital and largest city of England and the United Kingdom, with a population of 9.1 million people in 2024.
